title: hydnocarpus tomentosus
intro: Hydnocarpus tomentosus, also known as the chaulmoogra tree, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Flacourtiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani practices, and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Hydnocarpus tomentosus include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and psoriasis, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of rheumatism. In herbal preparations, the plant's seeds are often used to make oils and ointments that can be applied topically to soothe and heal the skin.
